title: Report on Participation in Disaster Mock Drill
institution: Manikaka Topawala Institute of Nursing, CHARUSAT – Community Health Nursing

fields.label: Duration
fields.hint: e.g. 2 hours, Half day

fields.label: Type of Disaster Simulated
fields.hint: e.g. Earthquake, Flood, Fire, Mass Casualty Incident, Chemical Spill

fields.label: Organizing Authority
fields.hint: e.g. District Health Authority, NDRF, Hospital Administration

fields.label: Departments / Agencies Involved
fields.hint: e.g. Fire Department, Police, Health Team, Ambulance Services, NGOs

fields.label: General Objective
fields.hint: State the broad overall goal of participating in and observing the disaster mock drill

fields.label: Specific Objectives
fields.hint: List the specific, measurable objectives of the mock drill observation

fields.label: Briefing Given by Experts
fields.hint: Describe the briefing session conducted before the drill — who briefed, what was covered, and how roles were explained

fields.label: Allocation of Roles and Responsibilities
fields.hint: Describe how roles and responsibilities were assigned to different teams and individuals before the drill

fields.label: Equipment and Logistics Arranged
fields.hint: List and describe the equipment, supplies, vehicles, and logistics arranged for the drill

fields.label: Safety Precautions Ensured
fields.hint: Describe the safety measures put in place before and during the mock drill

sections.section: E. Step-Wise Description of Mock Drill

fields.label: Disaster Scenario Initiation
fields.hint: Describe how the disaster scenario was initiated — the simulated event, trigger, and initial conditions

fields.label: Alert and Communication System
fields.hint: Describe the alert mechanisms used — sirens, radio, phone trees, public announcement systems, and how information was communicated

fields.label: Initial Response by Team
fields.hint: Describe the immediate actions taken by the response teams upon receiving the alert

fields.label: Rescue and Evacuation Process
fields.hint: Describe how rescue operations were carried out and how victims were evacuated from the simulated disaster site

fields.label: Triage and Patient Management
fields.hint: Describe the triage system used (e.g. START triage), how patients were categorized, and the care provided at the scene

fields.label: Referral / Transportation System
fields.hint: Describe how patients were transported and referred to health facilities during the drill

fields.label: Interdepartmental Coordination
fields.hint: Describe how different departments and agencies coordinated with each other throughout the drill

sections.section: F. Roles and Responsibilities of Different Teams

fields.label: Health Team
fields.hint: Describe the roles and responsibilities performed by the health team — doctors, nurses, paramedics, ANMs, etc.

fields.label: Fire / Rescue Team
fields.hint: Describe the roles and responsibilities of the fire and rescue team during the drill

fields.label: Police / Security
fields.hint: Describe the roles of the police and security personnel in crowd control, perimeter management, and coordination

fields.label: Ambulance Services
fields.hint: Describe the role of ambulance services in patient transport and pre-hospital care during the drill

fields.label: Others (If Any)
fields.hint: Describe the roles of any other agencies or teams involved — e.g. NGOs, Civil Defence, NDRF, volunteers

fields.label: Communication Effectiveness
fields.hint: Analyze how effectively communication was carried out between teams, command centres, and the public during the drill

fields.label: Team Coordination
fields.hint: Analyze how well teams coordinated with each other and how smoothly the overall response was executed

fields.label: Use of Equipment
fields.hint: Describe how effectively equipment and resources were utilized during the drill

fields.label: Time Management
fields.hint: Analyze how well time was managed — response time, evacuation time, triage speed, and overall drill duration

fields.label: Strengths of the Drill
fields.hint: List and describe the key strengths and positive aspects observed during the mock drill

fields.label: Gaps / Limitations Identified
fields.hint: Identify and describe the gaps, weaknesses, or areas requiring improvement observed during the drill

fields.label: Knowledge Gained
fields.hint: Describe the new knowledge acquired from observing and participating in the disaster mock drill

fields.label: Skills Observed
fields.hint: Describe the clinical, technical, and interpersonal skills observed being practiced during the drill

fields.label: Importance of Disaster Preparedness
fields.hint: Reflect on why disaster preparedness is critical for communities and health systems based on what you observed

fields.label: Relevance to Nursing Practice
fields.hint: Describe how the learnings from this drill are relevant to your role and responsibilities as a community health nurse

fields.label: Conclusion
fields.hint: Summarize the overall experience — key takeaways from the drill, its effectiveness, and how participation in this mock drill will shape your approach to disaster nursing and community health preparedness
